title: [FOLDED → OOM-0008] Abort: `assert(!PyErr_Occurred())` in `specialize` (`specialize.c:364`)
notes: RETIRED 2026-06-25 — folded into OOM-0008. `specialize`@specialize.c:364's `assert(!PyErr_Occurred())` is a GENERIC stale-pending-exception DETECTOR, not the bug. rr reverse-execution of the repro (`optparse.ngettext` + the load-bearing `import asyncio`) watched `tstate->current_exception` and reversed to where the stale MemoryError was set: `_PyErr_SetRaisedException` <- `_PyErr_NoMemory` <- `_PyObject_GC_NewVar`(PyFrameObject) <- `_PyFrame_MakeAndSetFrameObject`/`PyFrame_GetBack` <- `frame_back_get_impl` (reading frame.f_back). That is EXACTLY OOM-0008's bug: PyFrame_GetBack swallows a MemoryError under OOM (returns NULL without propagating; frame_back_get_impl maps NULL->None), leaving the exception pending so the NEXT `!PyErr_Occurred()` checkpoint aborts. OOM-0008 catches it at the type-cache lookup (typeobject.c:6343); this repro catches it at the LOAD_ATTR specializer (specialize.c:364) -- same producer, different detector (the OOM-0011 flakiness/drift between :364 and :6343 was always the same f_back-swallow). All of OOM-0011's vehicles (optparse/bdb/argparse/gettext) are frame-walkers that read f_back, like OOM-0008's 16. KEY LIMITATION: stale-exception crashes are backtrace-undisambiguable (the producer f_back already returned before the assert; it is NOT on the crash stack), so dedup can only key the DETECTOR assert site. OOM-0008 now owns BOTH detector sites (typeobject.c:6343 and specialize.c:364 + specialize_module_load_attr_lock_held + _Py_Specialize_LoadAttr, moved here); a stale-exception crash at either -> OOM-0008. AUDIT CAVEAT: these are producer-agnostic detectors -- almost always f_back-swallow (OOM-0008), but a stale exception from a DIFFERENT producer reaching them would mislabel; rr-confirm if behavior seems off. Gen scripts skip status==folded. Gist 892b6161 should be marked superseded. id NOT reused.
